logo_chatgpt_raster.png.q: Does the wordmark read EXACTLY 'Emberleaf Teas' (correct spelling, two words, no extra or missing letters)?
logo_chatgpt_raster.png.guidance: Read the text under the emblem letter by letter: E-m-b-e-r-l-e-a-f on line 1, T-e-a-s on line 2. Reject any misspelling, dropped letter, or invented text.

logo_chatgpt_raster.png.q: Does the emblem show a stylized tea leaf / small tea plant (green leaves) rising from a terracotta cup-or-pot base, matching an artisanal-tea brand?
logo_chatgpt_raster.png.guidance: Expect a sprig of pointed green leaves growing out of a curved terracotta/orange tulip-shaped vessel. On-subject for a loose-leaf tea company.

logo_chatgpt_raster.png.q: Is the artwork flat and solid-filled (no gradients, no drop shadows, no photographic elements)?
logo_chatgpt_raster.png.guidance: 5 = fully flat solid color shapes suitable for clean vectorizing; lower if you see gradients, glows, shadows, or photo texture. A slight AI-softness at edges is acceptable.

logo_chatgpt_raster.png.q: Do the colours match the brand palette: ember green (#4A5F4B) leaves/text and terracotta (#C77C5F) base on a plain near-white background?
logo_chatgpt_raster.png.guidance: 5 = green and terracotta clearly in-palette on white; lower if hues drift (too bright/teal/brown) or extra unrelated colours appear.

logo_chatgpt_raster.png.q: Is the composition centered with the emblem above the wordmark, brand name as the only text, on a plain white background?
logo_chatgpt_raster.png.guidance: 5 = centered, emblem-over-wordmark, no tagline or extra text, white field. Lower for off-center, clutter, or extra copy.

logo_chatgpt_raster.png.q: Free note: anything off about the generated input logo (artifacts, odd letterforms, palette drift)?
logo_chatgpt_raster.png.guidance: One short line. Note e.g. soft/fuzzy edges, leaf count, any color that looks off-brand.
logo_chatgpt_raster.png.ideal: minor AI softness only; otherwise on-brief

logo_vectorized.svg.q: Is the output a genuine vector (SVG built from <path> shapes, NOT just an embedded raster image wrapped in SVG)?
logo_vectorized.svg.guidance: Confirm the file contains many <path d=...> elements and NO <image> tag embedding the PNG. This SVG is all paths = genuine vector.

logo_vectorized.svg.q: Are the emblem's shapes/edges sharp and faithful to the original (leaf sprig + terracotta base recognizable, contours clean)?
logo_vectorized.svg.guidance: 5 = crisp curves matching the leaves and cup with no blobby distortion; lower if shapes are warped, merged, or jagged. Compare against the input emblem.

logo_vectorized.svg.q: Is the 'Emberleaf Teas' wordmark still fully legible and correctly spelled in the vector?
logo_vectorized.svg.guidance: Each letter should be a clean closed shape; read the full text. 5 = perfectly legible, correct spelling; lower if letters are broken, filled-in, or fused.

logo_vectorized.svg.q: Are the brand colours preserved (green leaves/text near #4A5F4B, terracotta base near #C77C5F) without introducing a different palette?
logo_vectorized.svg.guidance: Greens trace to ~rgb(94,112,93) and terracotta to ~rgb(200,138,103) here — close to brand but slightly light/desaturated green and slightly lighter terracotta. 5 = visually on-brand; dock for noticeable hue shift.

logo_vectorized.svg.q: Did vectorizing the deliberately-softened raster introduce stray anti-alias fringe shapes (thin halo-coloured slivers around edges)?
logo_vectorized.svg.guidance: Look for extra tiny paths in pale tints like rgb(222,234,220)/rgb(232,242,229) or muddy rgb(167,116,84) hugging the emblem outline — these are traced soft-edge halos. Any present = Yes (a defect).

logo_vectorized.svg.q: Is the colour count kept tight (~3-5 flat colours as the brief requires) rather than ballooning into many near-duplicate fills?
logo_vectorized.svg.guidance: Count distinct fills. 5 = ~3-5 clean colours; lower the more redundant near-greens/edge tints appear. This trace adds several intermediate fringe colours, so expect a mid score.

logo_vectorized.svg.q: Would this SVG be clean enough to hand to a printer as a scalable logo (no leftover background fill or transparency issues around the artwork)?
logo_vectorized.svg.guidance: Note the full canvas is a near-white rgb(254,254,254) background path rather than transparent; judge whether that and the edge slivers would need cleanup before print delivery.

task_rubric.q: Is the input to output pairing coherent and clearly on-task (raster Emberleaf Teas logo in to scalable vector SVG of the same logo out)?
task_rubric.guidance: 5 = output is unmistakably a vectorized version of the same emblem and wordmark. Lower if content drifted or the wrong asset was produced.

task_rubric.q: Would this output satisfy the client's brief (refine the ChatGPT logo into a sharp, print-ready vector with accurate colour)?
task_rubric.guidance: Weigh sharp edges + true vector (good) against the soft-edge fringe shapes, light green hue, and non-transparent background (would likely need touch-up). 5 = ship as-is; 3-4 = good but needs minor cleanup.

task_rubric.q: Would you accept and deliver this SVG to the client as the final refined logo?
task_rubric.guidance: Yes only if legibility, shapes, and colour are all acceptable and artifacts are negligible/easily cleaned; No if fringe slivers or palette drift are bad enough to reject.

task_rubric.q: Notes / what's missing for a fully print-ready deliverable.
task_rubric.guidance: One short line, e.g. remove anti-alias fringe paths, nudge green toward #4A5F4B, make background transparent, also supply AI/EPS + hi-res PNG promised in the brief.
task_rubric.ideal: minor cleanup of edge slivers and background; otherwise faithful
